page: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The configuration provides 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. A notable hardware characteristic is the massive memory allocation of 256.0 GB, yielding 32.0 GB of RAM per core. It also includes 1890 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2051.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.24 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7780.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 7988.12. Memory bandwidth tests show 622.38 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, but uncached large-block reads drop to 45.55 GB/sec. Passmark memory testing indicates a relative weakness, scoring 1716.72, which falls into the bottom 25%.

This server exhibits a high resource density of memory relative to compute cores, making it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. However, the low core count limits multi-threaded throughput, as seen in the weak performance for larger LLM inference tasks, such as 7B model text generation. The combination of 256 GB RAM, 1890 GB of local storage, and strong single-core execution makes this instance suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and text processing applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine configured with an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 4 physical cores and 8 dedicated vCPUs. The server features an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio, offering 256.0 GB of RAM alongside 1890 GB of local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2051.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 31.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling is moderate, and Passmark memory scores fall in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the server highly efficient for memory-intensive, low-core workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and text processing, while representing a tradeoff for highly parallelized compute tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6 virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, offering strong single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v6 is an Edsv6 VM with 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine pairing dedicated Intel Xeon processing with substantial local storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Extremely high memory density of 256 GB RAM yielding 32 GB per core
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and text processing
Tradeoff of lower multi-core scaling and weak large-block memory performance
